_comment: Investor / LP Update deck — Tiny house style. Synthetic DEMO data for Northwind Holdings (DEMO). Do not use as real figures.
title: Northwind Holdings — Investor Update

slides.title: The Compounding Thesis
slides.subtitle: How a portfolio of durable, cash-generative software businesses compounds value over time.

slides.title: Performance Snapshot
commentary: Revenue up double digits. Broad-based gains led by Acme and Globex, with no single company above a third of the mix.
EBITDA outgrew revenue. Operating leverage and a richer recurring mix lifted margin three points to 27%.
Cash conversion stayed high. ~77% of Adj. EBITDA converted to free cash flow, funding reinvestment and buybacks.
slides.footnote: Synthetic illustrative figures for Northwind Holdings (DEMO). Not actual results.

slides.title: Why We Compound
slides.lead: Three things make this portfolio compound rather than merely grow.
bullets: Durable, cash-generative software. Subscription and marketplace models with sticky customers, high gross margins, and recurring revenue that funds itself.
Decentralized operations. Each company runs its own P&L with its own leadership; head office allocates capital, not day-to-day decisions.
Disciplined capital allocation. Free cash flow is redeployed only where returns clear our hurdle — reinvestment, accretive acquisitions, debt paydown, or buybacks.
Permanent ownership horizon. No fund clock forces a sale. We hold quality businesses and let them compound across decades.

slides.title: Rising Earnings
commentary: Adj. EBITDA has grown for nine straight quarters to a $8.6M quarterly run-rate — a $31M LTM figure.
Compounding comes from steady reinvestment, not one-off spikes.

slides.title: Diversification
commentary: Revenue is spread across six companies — the largest, Acme, is under 27% of the total.
Diversification smooths cash flow and lowers single-company risk.

slides.title: Portfolio Spotlights
commentary: Each company is profitable and self-funding; none depends on outside capital to grow.

slides.title: NAV / Sum-of-the-Parts
commentary: Segment EBITDA × peer multiples implies an aggregate enterprise value near $280M.
Net of ~$110M illustrative debt, implied equity value is roughly $170M — a NAV-per-share well above where the stock trades.
All figures illustrative; not a valuation opinion.
slides.note: Implied EV by segment, $M. Illustrative sum-of-the-parts.

slides.title: Capital Allocation
commentary: Reinvestment comes first. Internal projects clearing our hurdle rate get funded before anything leaves the building.
Acquisitions are accretive and disciplined. We add businesses that fit the model at prices that beat our cost of capital.
Debt paydown and buybacks soak up the rest. When the share price lags NAV, repurchasing our own stock is the highest-return option available.
slides.footnote: Illustrative LTM uses of free cash flow. Percentages of ~$24M FCF.

slides.title: Thank you
slides.subtitle: Questions and discussion welcome.
